human_note: Demo-only retrieval expectations for the scripted propane conversations. Customer turns drive surfacing; matched evidence should stay short and explainable instead of replaying whole transcript turns.

trigger_rules.translation_rule: When the customer turn is non-English, keep the original customer text excerpt and include normalizedText for the translated/normalized evidence shown to the rep or used by backend scoring.
trigger_rules.ranking_rule: When two cards match the same turn, emit the operationally urgent or primary customer intent first and the supporting card second.

minimal_event_shape.compatibility_note: Build on the current KnowledgeCardEvent/KnowledgeCard contract by adding citation metadata, rank, and matchedEvidence per card; do not implement retrieval logic in this artifact.

top_level_fields.purpose: Immutable event identifier for the knowledge-card emission.
Keep value knowledge_cards for contract consistency.
Monotonic per-stream order within the call.
Links the card emission to the customer utterance window that triggered it.
Direct transcript correlation for replay and debugging.
Transcript-order correlation when event IDs are not enough.
Ranked knowledge cards to display for the turn.

card_fields.purpose: Stable knowledge item ID from the JSONL corpus.
Short answer/guidance text shown in the UI.
Portable source URI carried through from the corpus.
Rep-visible provenance label even when sourceUrl is synthetic://.
Human-readable playbook section for trust and debugging.
Minimal evidence snippets that explain why the card surfaced.

matched_evidence_fields.purpose: Why this evidence matched: trigger_phrase, keyword, translated_phrase, or translated_keyword.
Short excerpt from the customer utterance.
Translated/normalized excerpt when the original text is non-English or noisy.
Trigger phrase or keyword from the knowledge item that fired.
Locale of the customer excerpt carried into the event.

trigger_expectations.speaker: customer
trigger_expectations.utterance_excerpt: You missed my delivery yesterday, and I took off work for that window.

expected_cards.why_now: Company-caused missed stop plus inconvenience/frustration should surface the recovery play immediately.

matched_evidence.transcript_text: I took off work for that window
matched_evidence.normalized_text: I took off work for that window
matched_evidence.matched_knowledge_text: I took off work for this

trigger_expectations.speaker: customer
trigger_expectations.utterance_excerpt: Me llegó un volante de NorthStar Propane con un precio mucho más bajo, así que sí estoy pensando cambiarme.

expected_cards.why_now: Spanish competitor-pricing mention should immediately surface the retention save card with translation-backed evidence.

matched_evidence.matched_knowledge_text: me llegó un volante más barato

trigger_expectations.speaker: customer
trigger_expectations.utterance_excerpt: My winter bills keep jumping around. I need something steadier if I'm going to stay.

expected_cards.why_now: Billing volatility plus a request for steadier payments should surface budget billing before the rep responds.

trigger_expectations.speaker: customer
trigger_expectations.utterance_excerpt: My gauge is down to 15 percent, and I really do not want to run out before the weekend.

expected_cards.why_now: Low-but-not-empty urgency is the primary intent and should surface immediately.

matched_evidence.transcript_text: do not want to run out
matched_evidence.normalized_text: do not want to run out
matched_evidence.matched_knowledge_text: I don't want to run out

trigger_expectations.speaker: customer
trigger_expectations.utterance_excerpt: The storm pushed my last stop back, so I need to know whether Friday morning is really available or if I should expect another delay.

expected_cards.why_now: Operational delay anxiety is primary because the customer is asking whether the route changed again.

matched_evidence.transcript_text: The storm pushed my last stop back
matched_evidence.normalized_text: The storm pushed my last stop back

expected_cards.why_now: The same utterance also asks for a concrete delivery window, so the scheduling card should appear as supporting guidance.

matched_evidence.transcript_text: whether Friday morning is really available
matched_evidence.normalized_text: whether Friday morning is really available
matched_evidence.matched_knowledge_text: can you schedule it for Friday morning

trigger_expectations.speaker: customer
trigger_expectations.utterance_excerpt: What's the difference between will-call and keep-full again?

expected_cards.why_now: The customer explicitly asks for plan comparison and signals fatigue with gauge monitoring.

trigger_expectations.speaker: customer
trigger_expectations.utterance_excerpt: My renewal notice came in higher than last year, and if this is the new rate I may need to leave.

expected_cards.why_now: Renewal-price objection is the primary intent and should surface before the rep defends pricing.

trigger_expectations.speaker: customer
trigger_expectations.utterance_excerpt: I also cannot pay this whole balance today. Winter hit us hard, so I need a little more time and something steadier going forward.

expected_cards.why_now: Immediate payment hardship is the urgent operational need and should rank ahead of future billing stability.

matched_evidence.transcript_text: cannot pay this whole balance today
matched_evidence.normalized_text: cannot pay this whole balance today
matched_evidence.matched_knowledge_text: I can't pay all of this today

expected_cards.why_now: The same turn asks for steadier future payments, so budget billing should appear as the supporting option.

trigger_expectations.speaker: customer
trigger_expectations.utterance_excerpt: If there is a loyalty option on the renewal and I can split the balance that way, I'd rather stay than move the account.

expected_cards.why_now: The customer is still negotiating the renewal itself, so the renewal guidance stays primary.
Balance-splitting remains active in the same utterance and should stay visible as the closing support card.
